frameworks.name: multivariate pattern classification
frameworks.one_line: decode distributed CNS/ANS activity patterns into discrete emotional states

frameworks.name: theoretical models of emotion
frameworks.one_line: discrete-emotion vs dimensional accounts whose predictions MVPA can adjudicate

core_question: Can multivariate pattern classification of central and autonomic nervous system activity reliably identify emotion-specific biomarkers and test predictions of competing emotion theories?
key_assumptions: distinct emotional states leave reliable multivariate signatures in CNS/ANS activity
distributed pattern information is more diagnostic of emotion than univariate mean responses
emotion theories make discriminable predictions about pattern structure
manipulations: review-level contrast between univariate and multivariate analytic frames
comparison across CNS vs autonomic signal modalities as classifier inputs
key_findings: initial MVPA studies decode CNS and ANS activity into distinct emotional states with reliable accuracy
identifying single-marker autonomic/neural signatures of emotion remains elusive under traditional approaches
interpretations: MVPA offers an advantageous framework for emotion-specific biomarker discovery
pattern classification can serve as a tool for adjudicating between theoretical models of emotion
limitations_noted: initial studies only — generalisability of decoded patterns across contexts not yet established
